page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose virtual server instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it operates on an x86_64 architecture with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core tests. However, its multi-core capabilities are limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory performance is also constrained; memory bandwidth for small block read-only operations is in the bottom 10%, while large block read and write bandwidths reside in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and strong single-core execution, the ecs.g7a.large offers a stable compute environment for workloads that do not heavily parallelize. However, the low multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth present clear tradeoffs for data-intensive or highly concurrent applications, as reflected in the low database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents an entry-level dedicated resource tier suitable for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor at 2.55 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance does not include local storage or GPU resources, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25% for single-core stress-ng tests. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. This performance profile makes the instance highly suitable for single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving, while presenting clear performance limitations for highly parallelized applications, database operations, or memory-intensive workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, offering strong single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
